feedingProfile.preferredNpk: high-potassium (low-N fruiting feed, e.g. tomato feed); side-dress calcium nitrate 15.5-0-0 in-ground

commonIssues: Blossom-end rot from inconsistent soil moisture / poor calcium uptake — keep moisture even.
Flower/bud drop in hot days-and-nights or under drought stress.
Sunscald on fruit borne on small or sparsely-foliaged plants.
Over-fertilizing (too much nitrogen) yields lush foliage but fewer flowers and fruit.
Container plants dry out fast and may need daily watering in hot weather; minimum ~19 L (5 gal) pot recommended.
Aphids, cutworms, hornworms, bacterial leaf spot, and (greenhouse) spider mite / whitefly / grey mould.
Heavy fruit set can break stems; staking or caning recommended.

source.citation: UMN Extension — Growing peppers. Spacing 18 in apart; soak soil thoroughly at least once a week / ~1 in rain weekly; transplant after night lows above 50°F (10°C), avoid <60°F or >70°F; blossom-end rot, flower drop, sunscald, aphids/cutworms/hornworms.

Clemson HGIC — Pepper. Optimal day 70-85°F (21-29°C), night 60-70°F (16-21°C); uniform moisture, moisten soil ≥6 in deep, do not let soil dry between waterings; side-dress 3-4 weeks after planting then again 3-4 weeks later (calcium nitrate 15.5-0-0); over-fertilizing reduces blooms; blossom-end rot, bacterial leaf spot, anthracnose; 70-85 days to harvest from transplant.

RHS — How to grow sweet peppers. Containers at least 30 cm wide per plant; once flowering, feed weekly with high-potassium liquid fertiliser; tolerates min ~12°C, prefers ≥15°C, fruiting reduced above 30°C; container plants may need daily watering in hot, dry weather; bamboo-cane support for heavy fruit; aphids, blossom-end rot, spider mite, whitefly, grey mould.

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Growing Bell Peppers. Full sun, 6-8 hours of direct sun per day; grow in well-drained soil or containers (5-gallon ≈ 19 L minimum); consistent soil moisture critical for fruit set; vulnerable to blossom-end rot.

Confidence note — wateringProfile.baseIntervalDays (3 days) and hotWeatherMultiplier (2): authoritative sources specify outcome (even moisture, ~1 in/week soaking, daily in hot weather for containers) rather than a fixed day interval. The 3-day base is a conservative interpretation of 'soak at least weekly' plus 'do not let soil dry between waterings' for typical conditions; multiplier reflects RHS 'may need daily watering in hot, dry weather'. feedingProfile.baseIntervalDays (21) maps Clemson's 3-4 week side-dress cadence to a conservative 21 days.
